title: Pancreator self-development version recommendation
summary: A Pancreator self-development release packet MUST recommend a major increment, minor increment, or no version change and preserve the release-to-commit index boundary.
instructions: This policy MUST apply only when the active installation is explicitly marked as Pancreator self-development in project.json.

The release steward MUST read VERSION and release/index.json, then populate release.versioning with current_version, recommendation, proposed_version, rationale, compatibility, and release_index_action.
recommendation MUST be exactly major, minor, or neither.

The release steward MUST recommend major when the completed work introduces a backward-incompatible installed layout, command contract, durable-state schema, governance contract, or update path that requires target migration.
The release steward MUST recommend minor when the completed work adds a backward-compatible operator capability, workflow, persona, validation behavior, or material installation behavior.
The release steward MUST recommend neither for documentation, tests, internal refactors, and bounded fixes that do not materially change the installed or operator-facing contract.
For major, proposed_version MUST be the next <major+1>.0 version; for minor, it MUST be <major>.<minor+1>; for neither, it MUST preserve the current version.
The release steward MUST NOT edit VERSION, release/index.json, create a commit, or invent a commit hash.
The operator MUST create the release commit containing the exact installable payload before adding its version-to-commit mapping to release/index.json in a separate metadata commit.
release_index_action MUST describe both operator actions and MUST NOT imply that the release commit can contain its own hash.
